circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: The Yas Marina Circuit, a 5. 281 km track on Abu Dhabi's Yas Island designed by Hermann Tilke, has served as the venue for Formula One's season-closing Abu Dhabi Grand Prix since 2009. The circuit became the second Middle Eastern Formula One venue, following Bahrain and preceding later additions in Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: The Albert Park Circuit is a street course encircling Albert Park Lake in Melbourne that hosts the annual Formula One Australian Grand Prix along with various supporting motorsport events. As an FIA Grade 1 licensed facility, the track meets the highest international standards for grand prix racing.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Austria, this 4. 326 km circuit was originally established as the Österreichring and staged the Austrian Grand Prix throughout the 1970s and 1980s. Following modifications and a renaming, the venue resumed hosting the championship race from 1997 to 2003.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street track situated in Azerbaijan's capital along the Baku Boulevard, spanning 6. 003 km per lap to rank as the fourth-longest venue in Formula One. After hosting the 2016 European Grand Prix as its inaugural F1 event, the circuit established its own championship race, the Azerbaijan Grand Prix, which debuted in 2017.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, the 5. 412 km Bahrain International Circuit hosts the annual Formula One Grand Prix, which was the first F1 race held in the Middle East. The FIA Grade 1 licensed facility accommodates various motorsport categories including GP2 racing, drag racing, and 24-hour endurance competitions, with multiple track configurations available.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya stretches 4. 657 kilometers and features a versatile mix of long straights and diverse corner configurations that test multiple racing disciplines. The venue holds an FIA Grade 1 license and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: Located near the Belgian town of Spa, this 7 km circuit in Wallonia has been a fixture of Formula One racing since the sport's inaugural 1950 season. The venue continues to host the Belgian Grand Prix annually, with only two exceptions in 2003 and 2006, maintaining its status as one of motorsport's most historic and enduring racing facilities.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, this 4. 361-kilometre circuit serves as the home of Canada's Formula One Grand Prix. The track has a rich racing heritage, having previously accommodated various international motorsport series including sports car championships and North American racing competitions.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: Located in Shanghai's Jiading District, the Shanghai International Circuit is a prominent motorsport venue that hosted the Chinese Grand Prix biennially from 2004 until 2019, resuming in 2024, while also accommodating electric racing events from 2024 onward.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Silverstone is an English racing circuit situated in the Northamptonshire region that has served as the traditional venue for the British Grand Prix since 1948. The track gained historical significance by hosting the inaugural World Championship race in 1950, though it shared hosting duties with Aintree and Brands Hatch between 1955 and 1986 before becoming the permanent home of the British Grand Prix from 1987 onwards. Beyond Formula One, the circuit also accommodates major motorcycle racing events.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring, a 4. 381 km circuit located in Mogyoród near Budapest, hosted the inaugural Formula One Grand Prix held within the Eastern Bloc in 1986. Built in just eight months, a record pace for Formula One venues, the track was constructed outside the city following government preference over a proposed street circuit in Budapest's central park.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: The Autodromo Nazionale Monza is a 5. 793 km Italian racing facility constructed in 1922 near Milan, making it mainland Europe's oldest purpose-built circuit and only the third of its kind globally. Renowned as "the Temple of Speed" for its extended straights and quick corners, it has continuously hosted the Italian Grand Prix since 1949, except for a 1980 closure during renovations.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit is a 5. 807-kilometer Japanese motorsport venue operated by Honda Mobilityland in Mie Prefecture, with a spectator capacity of 155,000. The track gained prominence as a regular Formula One Grand Prix host and also features in the Japanese Super Formula championship series.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street track that navigates through Paradise, Nevada, weaving past the city's most iconic establishments along Las Vegas Boulevard. The course incorporates renowned landmarks such as the MSG Sphere, Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas as it traverses the famous hotel and casino district.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: The Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez is a 4. 304 km circuit in Mexico City, Mexico, honoring two racing brothers who died in accidents during the 1960s and 1970s. The venue has hosted the Formula One Mexican Grand Prix twice before, most recently in 1992 on a different track configuration, and resumed holding the race in 2015 under its current Mexico City Grand Prix designation since 2021.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary street circuit constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, Florida, stretching 3. 363 miles with 19 corners and an average qualifying speed of approximately 139 mph. Designed by specialist Formula One architects, the track was purpose-built to host the Miami Grand Prix when it joined the championship calendar in 2022.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco is a 3. 337 km street-based track that winds through the urban streets of Monte Carlo and La Condamine, encircling Monaco's harbour. Though technically spanning multiple neighbourhoods, it is widely known as the Monte Carlo circuit due to its primary location within that district.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: Located in the Dutch dunes near the North Sea, this 4. 259 km circuit situated west of Amsterdam regained its place on the Formula One calendar in 2021 with the Dutch Grand Prix. The venue will continue hosting the grand prix through 2026, after which its F1 partnership concludes.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: The Lusail International Circuit is situated near Lusail in northern Qatar and features a 5. 419-kilometer layout with a 1. 068-kilometer main straight.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Interlagos is a 4. 309 km motorsport facility in São Paulo, Brazil, originally opened in 1940 and renamed in 1985 to commemorate late Formula One driver José Carlos Pace, who tragically died in an aviation accident eight years earlier. The counterclockwise circuit hosts major racing events and features an associated kart track dedicated to another Brazilian racing legend, Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: The Jeddah Corniche Circuit is a Saudi Arabian street track positioned along the Red Sea waterfront that has hosted the Formula One Saudi Arabian Grand Prix since its inaugural event in 2021.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit is a street-based racing venue located around Singapore's Marina Bay, incorporating sections of the Downtown Core and Kallang planning districts.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, this 4. 657-kilometer Spanish circuit features a versatile design combining extended straights with diverse corner configurations, making it suitable for testing all aspects of car performance. The track, which holds FIA Grade 1 certification, accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: Located near Austin, Texas, the Circuit of the Americas is a 3. 426-mile Grade 1 FIA circuit that hosts multiple racing series, most prominently the United States Grand Prix. Beyond Formula One, the venue serves as home to NASCAR events, the MotoGP Americas round, and the Lone Star Le Mans endurance race, while previously accommodating various other motorsport competitions including IndyCar and sports car championships.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Thai-British driver Alexander Albon represents Thailand in Formula One racing, currently competing for the Williams team.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Spanish driver Fernando Alonso currently races for Aston Martin in Formula One, where he has claimed two world championship titles in 2005 and 2006 during his tenure with Renault, along with 32 Grand Prix victories. Beyond single-seaters, Alonso has achieved considerable success in endurance racing, including two Le Mans victories with Toyota and the 2018-19 World Endurance Championship, making him the sole driver to win both a Formula One drivers' title and a World Endurance drivers' championship.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli competes for Mercedes in Formula One, having secured six race victories following his 2025 debut in the sport.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Haas. He competes at the highest level of motorsport as part of the team's driver lineup.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Brazilian driver Gabriel Bortoleto currently races in Formula One for the Audi team.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Finnish driver Valtteri Bottas currently races for Cadillac in Formula One, bringing extensive experience from his 13 seasons in the sport. He achieved two consecutive World Drivers' Championship runner-up finishes with Mercedes in 2019 and 2020, and has accumulated 10 Grand Prix victories throughout his career.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Franco Colapinto is an Argentine motorsport competitor currently racing in Formula One with Alpine.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Jack Doohan, an Australian driver, competes in European endurance racing while maintaining a reserve role with the Haas Formula One team. He has participated in seven Grand Prix events between 2024 and 2025.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: French driver Pierre Gasly currently races for Alpine in Formula One after previously achieving victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix while driving for AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Hadjar is a French-Algerian racing driver who represents France in Formula One competition, currently driving for Red Bull Racing.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a British driver competing for Ferrari who has secured seven Formula One World Drivers' Championships, equalling Michael Schumacher's record. He holds multiple competitive records including the most race victories, pole positions, and podium finishes in the sport's history.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: German driver Nicolas Hülkenberg currently races in Formula One for Audi, bringing significant experience from his extensive career in motorsport. Beyond his single-seater commitments, Hülkenberg achieved notable success in endurance racing, securing victory at the prestigious 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 while competing for Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson is a New Zealand driver competing in Formula One with the Racing Bulls team.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: Charles Leclerc represents Monaco in Formula One racing with Ferrari, where he achieved second place in the 2022 drivers' championship standings. The Ferrari driver has accumulated nine race victories throughout his nine seasons competing at the sport's highest level.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Kevin Magnussen, a Danish driver, previously competed in Formula One across two separate periods between 2014 and 2024. He currently races in the FIA World Endurance Championship for BMW through WRT alongside part-time NASCAR commitments with Trackhouse Racing.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver, secured the 2025 Formula One World Drivers' Championship while racing for McLaren. Over his eight seasons in the sport, he has accumulated 11 Grand Prix victories.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon is a French Formula One driver currently competing for Haas. Earlier in his career, he achieved a Grand Prix victory at the 2021 Hungarian round while driving for Alpine.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Mexican driver Sergio Pérez currently races in Formula One for Cadillac. The veteran competitor finished as championship runner-up in 2023 while driving for Red Bull and has accumulated six Grand Prix victories throughout his 15-year career in the sport.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Oscar Piastri is an Australian Formula One driver currently racing for McLaren, having secured nine Grand Prix victories since joining the grid.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Australian driver Daniel Ricciardo, known by his "Honey Badger" moniker, competed in Formula One over a 14-year span from 2011 through 2024, securing victories in eight Grand Prix races during his career.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: British driver George Russell currently races for Mercedes in Formula One, having secured seven Grand Prix victories throughout his eight-season career in the sport.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz competes in Formula One for Williams, having accumulated four race victories throughout his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: Logan Sargeant is an American racing driver currently competing in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category with Proton Competition. He previously raced in Formula One during 2023 and 2024, and has also competed in IMSA's LMP2 class representing Era Motorsport.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian motorsport competitor who currently drives in Formula One for the Aston Martin team.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Yuki Tsunoda, a Japanese driver, has been involved in Formula One racing since 2021, currently serving as a reserve driver for both Red Bull Racing and Racing Bulls teams through 2025.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: Max Verstappen is a Dutch-Belgian driver competing for Red Bull Racing who has secured four consecutive world championships between 2021 and 2024. The accomplished pilot has claimed 71 Grand Prix victories throughout his 12-season Formula One career.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu is a Chinese racing driver currently employed as a reserve driver for Cadillac in Formula One, making him the sole competitor from China to have participated in the sport. He actively raced in the championship between 2022 and 2024 before transitioning to his reserve role.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: The Enstone-based team has competed as Alpine since 2021, representing Renault's rebranding of its works Formula One operation until the manufacturer's withdrawal at the end of 2025. Currently powered by Mercedes engines, Alpine's only victory came through Esteban Ocon at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix, with the squad now fielding Pierre Gasly and Franco Colapinto as its driver pairing.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: The current iteration of this British marque competes in Formula One under the AMR GP Limited banner, fielding cars badged as Aston Martin Aramco Formula One Team.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari, the racing arm of the Italian luxury automaker, stands as Formula One's longest-standing and most accomplished team, having entered every World Championship season since the sport's inception in 1950. The squad, informally called "the Prancing Horse" after its iconic emblem, remains a cornerstone of competitive motor racing.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: Gene Haas founded this American Formula One team in 2014, making its competitive debut in 2016. Under current team principal Ayao Komatsu, who took over after Guenther Steiner's departure in early 2024, the squad competes as TGR Haas F1 Team.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: Founded in 1970 by Peter Sauber as a Swiss engineering company, the organization initially excelled in endurance racing before joining Formula One in 1993 as an independent constructor. Over three decades in the sport, the team underwent numerous transformations and became the fourth-longest-serving constructor by race entries before its 2025 departure, when Audi acquired its operations to establish their own F1 program's technical foundation.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren Racing is a British Formula One constructor headquartered in Woking that competes as McLaren Mastercard F1 Team. Operating as part of the McLaren Group, the team is owned by Bahrain's Mumtalakat Holding Company.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes has maintained a continuous presence in Formula One since 1954, functioning as both a constructor and engine supplier. The current team, based in Brackley and operating under a German licence as Mercedes-AMG Petronas, became a tri-owned entity in January 2022 when chemical company Ineos joined the Mercedes-Benz Group and team principal Toto Wolff as equal partners. The marque is traditionally associated with the "Silver Arrows" nomenclature.

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that began competing in 2024, operating as a second team under Red Bull GmbH ownership alongside Red Bull Racing. Based in Faenza with operations near its sister team in Milton Keynes, the squad represents the automotive conglomerate's expansion into maintaining dual racing entries.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team established in 2024, operated by Red Bull GmbH alongside Red Bull Racing. Based in Faenza with operations near its Milton Keynes sister facility, the team functions as Red Bull's second constructor outfit in the championship.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Founded in 2005, Red Bull Racing operates as an Austrian-licensed Formula One team headquartered in England and owned by the Red Bull conglomerate. The team achieved considerable success under Christian Horner's management from its inception through 2024, before Laurent Mekies assumed leadership in 2025. Red Bull GmbH also owns Racing Bulls, making it one of two Formula One operations within the company's portfolio.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering, founded in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head following the collapse of an earlier Williams racing operation, stands as a British Formula One constructor and team. Headquartered in Grove, Oxfordshire, the outfit competes in the 2026 season as Atlassian Williams F1 Team.
